name: onboarding-refresh
goal: Refresh the onboarding experience with quick fixes for model defaults, messaging, and file creation (Phase 1), plus comprehensive overhaul of both CLI and conversational onboarding paths (Phase 2).

userStories.title: Update Default AI Tiers
userStories.description: Update outdated model names in onboard.ts (lines 381-383 and 444-446) to current versions using anthropic/ prefix format.
acceptanceCriteria: Both DEFAULT_AI_CONFIG and API_KEY_AI_CONFIG use current model names
Model names use anthropic/ prefix format (anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5, etc.)
npm run typecheck passes
Manual verification: arete install && cat arete.yaml shows correct tiers

userStories.title: Improve Onboard Messaging
userStories.description: Add context explaining WHY Areté needs AI credentials. Add info message before AI setup section and change credential update prompt to explain purpose.
acceptanceCriteria: Info message about Areté's AI usage appears before AI configuration section
Credential prompt explains purpose (not just 'Update Anthropic credentials?')
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add agent-observations.md to DEFAULT_FILES
userStories.description: Ensure agent-observations.md is created on workspace install by adding it to DEFAULT_FILES in packages/core/src/workspace-structure.ts (NOT install.ts).
acceptanceCriteria: Entry added to DEFAULT_FILES object in workspace-structure.ts
File created at .arete/memory/items/agent-observations.md with starter content
Fresh arete install creates the file
arete update on existing workspace adds the file if missing
npm run typecheck passes
npm test passes

userStories.title: Session-Start Context Injection
userStories.description: Add rule section telling agents to read observations, week focus, and commitments at conversation start. Update BOTH cursor and claude-code rule files.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/runtime/rules/cursor/agent-memory.mdc updated with Session Start section
packages/runtime/rules/claude-code/agent-memory.mdc updated with identical content
diff between the two files shows no unexpected differences
Section includes: observations (last 5), week.md check, commitments due this week

userStories.title: CLI Onboard Enhancement
userStories.description: Extend arete onboard to include calendar choice (Apple/Google/Skip), optional context prompts, and first win suggestions. Use service methods not subprocesses for integration.
acceptanceCriteria: Calendar choice prompt appears after AI credentials
Apple Calendar selection configures integration using services.integrations.configure()
Google Calendar selection runs OAuth flow then configures
Skip option works without error
Context prompts are optional (Enter to skip)
First win suggestions displayed at end
--json mode works (skips interactive prompts)
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Getting-Started Skill — Structural Updates
userStories.description: Update getting-started skill to reference current workspace structure. Remove 'areas' references, update now/ structure description, add planning skills info.
acceptanceCriteria: No references to 'areas' remain in skill
now/ structure correctly described (scratchpad.md, week.md, today.md)
Planning skills (week-plan, daily-plan) mentioned

userStories.title: Getting-Started Skill — Calendar Integration
userStories.description: Update skill to offer calendar choice (Apple vs Google) and run commands directly instead of delegating to CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: Calendar choice offered in Path C (Apple vs Google vs Skip)
Apple path runs arete integration configure apple-calendar --all via bash
Google path guides user through OAuth, waits for confirmation
--all flag used to avoid interactive calendar selection
Pull command runs after successful configuration

userStories.title: Getting-Started Skill — Discovery Questions
userStories.description: Simplify discovery questions for faster onboarding. Reduce to 3 focused questions with clear path routing.
acceptanceCriteria: Discovery questions are concise (Q1: data sources, Q2: immediate need, Q3: readiness)
Path routing is clear and deterministic
Each path leads to a concrete first win

userStories.title: Parity Verification
userStories.description: Verify both CLI and agent onboarding paths cover identity, AI credentials, calendar choice/setup, basic context, and first win.
acceptanceCriteria: All parity checklist items have coverage in both CLI and agent paths
No path leaves user stranded mid-onboarding
Any intentional differences documented
